text: A function \(f\) is increasing on an interval if:
explanation: \(f\) is increasing where \( f'(x) > 0 \) and decreasing where \( f'(x) < 0 \).
name: Increasing Function

text: At a local maximum, the second derivative satisfies:
explanation: Second Derivative Test: local max iff \( f'(x)=0 \) and \( f''(x)<0 \).
name: Second Derivative Test - Max

text: The equation of tangent to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
name: Equation of Tangent

text: The equation of normal to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
name: Equation of Normal

text: Rolle's theorem requires \(f\) to be continuous on \([a,b]\), differentiable on \((a,b)\), and:
explanation: Rolle's Theorem: if additionally \( f(a) = f(b) \), then \( \exists\, c\in(a,b) \) such that \( f'(c)=0 \).
name: Rolle's Theorem Condition

text: The Mean Value Theorem guarantees \( \exists\, c \in (a,b) \) such that:
name: Mean Value Theorem

text: The rate of change of area of circle w.r.t. radius when \(r=3\) cm is:
name: Rate of Change

text: A point of inflection occurs where:
explanation: Inflection point: \( f''(x) = 0 \) AND \( f''\) changes sign there (concavity changes).
name: Point of Inflection

text: The approximate value formula using differentials is:
explanation: Linear approximation: \( f(x+\Delta x) \approx f(x) + f'(x)\cdot\Delta x \)
name: Linear Approximation

text: The function \( f(x) = x^3 - 3x \) has local minimum at:
explanation: \( f'(x) = 3x^2-3=0 \Rightarrow x=\pm1 \). \( f''(1)=6>0 \Rightarrow \) local min at \(x=1\).
name: Local Minimum

text: If the side of a square increases at 2 cm/s, the rate of increase of area when side = 5 cm is:
name: Related Rates

text: The maximum value of \( f(x) = \sin x + \cos x \) is:
name: Maximum of sin+cos

text: The angle made by the tangent to \( y=x^2 \) at \( (1,1) \) with x-axis is:
name: Angle of Tangent

text: The curve \( y = f(x) \) is concave upward when:
explanation: Concave upward (cup shape) when \( f''(x) > 0 \). Concave downward when \( f''(x) < 0 \).
name: Concavity

text: The critical points of \( f(x) \) are points where:
explanation: Critical points: where \( f'(x) = 0 \) or \( f'(x) \) does not exist. Extrema can only occur at critical points.
name: Critical Points
